verses.text: Now these are the judgments which thou shalt set before them. Guidance: After the Ten Commandments comes the case law — specific situations that show how the principles play out in everyday life. God's covenant gets very practical.

If thou buy a Hebrew servant, six years he serves, the seventh he goes out free. If he came in by himself, he goes out by himself; if married, his wife with him. If his master gave him a wife who bore children, the wife and children stay; he goes alone. Guidance: Israelite servitude is bounded, not lifelong. Six years cap, automatic release. God's law puts a clock on bondage.

If thou buy a Hebrew servant, six years he serves, the seventh he goes out free. If he came in by himself, he goes out by himself; if married, his wife with him. If his master gave him a wife who bore children, the wife and children stay; he goes alone. Guidance: Israelite servitude is bounded, not lifelong. Six years cap, automatic release. God's law puts a clock on bondage.

If thou buy a Hebrew servant, six years he serves, the seventh he goes out free. If he came in by himself, he goes out by himself; if married, his wife with him. If his master gave him a wife who bore children, the wife and children stay; he goes alone. Guidance: Israelite servitude is bounded, not lifelong. Six years cap, automatic release. God's law puts a clock on bondage.

If the servant shall plainly say, I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free — his master shall bring him to the judges, to the door, and bore his ear through with an awl; he shall serve him for ever. Guidance: A bound ear, not a bound body. The servant who chooses to stay does so with a visible mark; love, not force, is what holds him.

If the servant shall plainly say, I love my master, my wife, and my children; I will not go out free — his master shall bring him to the judges, to the door, and bore his ear through with an awl; he shall serve him for ever. Guidance: A bound ear, not a bound body. The servant who chooses to stay does so with a visible mark; love, not force, is what holds him.

If a man sells his daughter as a maidservant, she shall not go out as the menservants do. If she does not please her master who betrothed her to himself, he shall let her be redeemed — he has no power to sell her to a foreign nation, because he has dealt deceitfully with her. Guidance: Even within a system the modern world rightly rejects, God's law gives daughters protections. Her dignity is protected by law.

If a man sells his daughter as a maidservant, she shall not go out as the menservants do. If she does not please her master who betrothed her to himself, he shall let her be redeemed — he has no power to sell her to a foreign nation, because he has dealt deceitfully with her. Guidance: Even within a system the modern world rightly rejects, God's law gives daughters protections. Her dignity is protected by law.

If he has betrothed her to his son, he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters. If he takes another wife, her food, clothing, and marriage rights shall not be diminished. If he denies these three, she shall go out free without money. Guidance: A woman in a household is owed food, clothing, and marital honor — non-negotiable. If denied, she walks free, no payment required. Three protections God writes into law for women.

If he has betrothed her to his son, he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters. If he takes another wife, her food, clothing, and marriage rights shall not be diminished. If he denies these three, she shall go out free without money. Guidance: A woman in a household is owed food, clothing, and marital honor — non-negotiable. If denied, she walks free, no payment required. Three protections God writes into law for women.

If he has betrothed her to his son, he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters. If he takes another wife, her food, clothing, and marriage rights shall not be diminished. If he denies these three, she shall go out free without money. Guidance: A woman in a household is owed food, clothing, and marital honor — non-negotiable. If denied, she walks free, no payment required. Three protections God writes into law for women.

He that smites a man so that he dies shall surely be put to death. If a man lies not in wait, but God delivers him into his hand, I will appoint a place where he shall flee. But if a man comes presumptuously to slay his neighbour — take him from Mine altar that he may die. Guidance: God distinguishes manslaughter from murder. Cities of refuge for the accidental killer; no asylum for premeditated violence. Intent matters.

He that smites a man so that he dies shall surely be put to death. If a man lies not in wait, but God delivers him into his hand, I will appoint a place where he shall flee. But if a man comes presumptuously to slay his neighbour — take him from Mine altar that he may die. Guidance: God distinguishes manslaughter from murder. Cities of refuge for the accidental killer; no asylum for premeditated violence. Intent matters.

He that smites a man so that he dies shall surely be put to death. If a man lies not in wait, but God delivers him into his hand, I will appoint a place where he shall flee. But if a man comes presumptuously to slay his neighbour — take him from Mine altar that he may die. Guidance: God distinguishes manslaughter from murder. Cities of refuge for the accidental killer; no asylum for premeditated violence. Intent matters.

He that smiteth his father, or his mother, shall be surely put to death. Guidance: Violence against parents shatters the foundation of the household. The fifth commandment is not optional; God protects fathers and mothers with the weight of capital law.

And he that stealeth a man, and selleth him, or if he be found in his hand, he shall surely be put to death. Guidance: Kidnapping for trafficking carries the death penalty in God's law. Three thousand years before modern anti-slavery movements, Scripture names human trafficking as a capital crime.

And he that curseth his father, or his mother, shall surely be put to death. Guidance: The Hebrew word for curse means to treat as light or contemptible. God's law treats verbal abuse of parents on par with physical violence — both wound the foundation.

If men strive and one smite another with a stone or fist, and he die not but keep his bed — if he rise again and walk abroad, the smiter shall be quit; only he shall pay for lost time and ensure he is thoroughly healed. Guidance: God's law requires the harm-doer to cover lost wages and medical bills until the victim is fully healed. Restitution is not a gesture; it is repair.

If men strive and one smite another with a stone or fist, and he die not but keep his bed — if he rise again and walk abroad, the smiter shall be quit; only he shall pay for lost time and ensure he is thoroughly healed. Guidance: God's law requires the harm-doer to cover lost wages and medical bills until the victim is fully healed. Restitution is not a gesture; it is repair.

If a man smite his servant or maid with a rod, and he die under his hand, he shall be surely punished. If the servant continue a day or two, the master is not punished, for he is his money. Guidance: Even within a slave system, the master is held legally accountable for the death of a servant. The protection is incomplete by modern standards, but it is a wedge against unchecked violence.

If a man smite his servant or maid with a rod, and he die under his hand, he shall be surely punished. If the servant continue a day or two, the master is not punished, for he is his money. Guidance: Even within a slave system, the master is held legally accountable for the death of a servant. The protection is incomplete by modern standards, but it is a wedge against unchecked violence.

If men strive and hurt a pregnant woman so her fruit depart, with no mischief — punished as her husband shall lay upon him. But if any mischief follow — life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot, burning for burning, wound for wound, stripe for stripe. Guidance: An eye for an eye limits revenge; it does not require it. Proportionality, not retaliation. Jesus later transforms this from personal mandate into divine one.

If men strive and hurt a pregnant woman so her fruit depart, with no mischief — punished as her husband shall lay upon him. But if any mischief follow — life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot, burning for burning, wound for wound, stripe for stripe. Guidance: An eye for an eye limits revenge; it does not require it. Proportionality, not retaliation. Jesus later transforms this from personal mandate into divine one.

If men strive and hurt a pregnant woman so her fruit depart, with no mischief — punished as her husband shall lay upon him. But if any mischief follow — life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot, burning for burning, wound for wound, stripe for stripe. Guidance: An eye for an eye limits revenge; it does not require it. Proportionality, not retaliation. Jesus later transforms this from personal mandate into divine one.

If men strive and hurt a pregnant woman so her fruit depart, with no mischief — punished as her husband shall lay upon him. But if any mischief follow — life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot, burning for burning, wound for wound, stripe for stripe. Guidance: An eye for an eye limits revenge; it does not require it. Proportionality, not retaliation. Jesus later transforms this from personal mandate into divine one.

If a man smite the eye of his servant or maid so it perish — he shall let them go free for the eye's sake. If he smite out a servant's tooth — he shall let them go free for the tooth's sake. Guidance: Even one wound to a servant cancels the bond. God's law makes a slave's body more sacred than the master's investment. Permanent injury earns immediate freedom.

If a man smite the eye of his servant or maid so it perish — he shall let them go free for the eye's sake. If he smite out a servant's tooth — he shall let them go free for the tooth's sake. Guidance: Even one wound to a servant cancels the bond. God's law makes a slave's body more sacred than the master's investment. Permanent injury earns immediate freedom.

If an ox gore a man or woman so they die, the ox shall be stoned; the owner is quit. But if the ox was wont to gore and the owner had not kept him in, and he killed someone — the ox is stoned, and the owner also put to death. Guidance: Negligence has consequences. A known-dangerous animal makes the owner culpable. God's law holds us responsible not only for what we did but for what we allowed.

If an ox gore a man or woman so they die, the ox shall be stoned; the owner is quit. But if the ox was wont to gore and the owner had not kept him in, and he killed someone — the ox is stoned, and the owner also put to death. Guidance: Negligence has consequences. A known-dangerous animal makes the owner culpable. God's law holds us responsible not only for what we did but for what we allowed.

If a ransom be laid upon him, he shall give for his life whatsoever is laid. Whether the ox gored son or daughter, by this judgment it shall be done. If the ox pushed a servant, the owner gives thirty shekels to their master, and the ox shall be stoned. Guidance: God's law equates the value of every child — son or daughter — and assigns a real but limited ransom for a servant's death. Each life has a price the law refuses to skip.

If a ransom be laid upon him, he shall give for his life whatsoever is laid. Whether the ox gored son or daughter, by this judgment it shall be done. If the ox pushed a servant, the owner gives thirty shekels to their master, and the ox shall be stoned. Guidance: God's law equates the value of every child — son or daughter — and assigns a real but limited ransom for a servant's death. Each life has a price the law refuses to skip.

If a ransom be laid upon him, he shall give for his life whatsoever is laid. Whether the ox gored son or daughter, by this judgment it shall be done. If the ox pushed a servant, the owner gives thirty shekels to their master, and the ox shall be stoned. Guidance: God's law equates the value of every child — son or daughter — and assigns a real but limited ransom for a servant's death. Each life has a price the law refuses to skip.

If a man open a pit or dig one and not cover it, and an ox or ass fall in, the owner of the pit shall make it good; he shall give money to the owner of the animal, and the dead beast shall be his. Guidance: God's law protects the unsuspecting. If you dig a hazard and leave it open, you pay for what falls in. Anticipate the harm your work can cause.

If a man open a pit or dig one and not cover it, and an ox or ass fall in, the owner of the pit shall make it good; he shall give money to the owner of the animal, and the dead beast shall be his. Guidance: God's law protects the unsuspecting. If you dig a hazard and leave it open, you pay for what falls in. Anticipate the harm your work can cause.

If one man's ox hurt another so it dies, they shall sell the live ox and divide the money; the dead ox also divide. If the ox was known to push and the owner had not kept him in, he shall pay ox for ox; the dead shall be his own. Guidance: Two animals collide, two owners share the loss equally. But a known-dangerous animal places the entire cost on the owner who didn't restrain it. Knowing is owing.

If one man's ox hurt another so it dies, they shall sell the live ox and divide the money; the dead ox also divide. If the ox was known to push and the owner had not kept him in, he shall pay ox for ox; the dead shall be his own. Guidance: Two animals collide, two owners share the loss equally. But a known-dangerous animal places the entire cost on the owner who didn't restrain it. Knowing is owing.
